2604.13950.title: Causal Drawbridges: Characterizing Gradient Blocking of Syntactic Islands in Transformer LMs
2604.13950.paper_summary: We show how causal interventions in Transformer models provide insights into English syntax by focusing on a long-standing challenge for syntactic theory: syntactic islands. Extraction from coordinated verb phrases is often degraded, yet acceptability varies gradiently with lexical content (e.g., "I know what he hates art and loves" vs. "I know what he looked down and saw"). We show that modern Transformer language models replicate human judgments across this gradient. Using causal interventions that isolate functionally relevant subspaces in Transformer blocks, attention modules, and MLPs, we demonstrate that extraction from coordination islands engages the same filler-gap mechanisms as canonical wh-dependencies, but that these mechanisms are selectively blocked to varying degrees. By projecting a large corpus of unrelated text onto these causally identified subspaces, we derive a novel linguistic hypothesis: the conjunction "and" is represented differently in extractable versus non-extractable constructions, corresponding to expressions encoding relational dependencies versus purely conjunctive uses. These results illustrate how mechanistic interpretability can inform syntax, generating new hypotheses about linguistic representation and processing.
2604.13950.paper_summary_zh: 我們展示了在 Transformer 模型中進行因果干預如何通過聚焦於語法理論的一個長期挑戰：語法島，來提供對英語語法的見解。從協調動詞短語中提取通常會受到損害，但可接受性隨著詞彙內容而漸變（例如，“我知道他討厭藝術並喜歡”與“我知道他低頭看到”）。我們表明，現代 Transformer 語言模型在這個漸變中複製了人類的判斷。通過使用因果干預來隔離 Transformer 區塊、注意模組和 MLP 中功能相關的子空間，我們展示了從協調島中提取涉及與典型 wh 依賴相同的填充-間隙機制，但這些機制在不同程度上被選擇性地阻塞。通過將大量無關文本投影到這些因果識別的子空間中，我們推導出一個新穎的語言假設：連詞“和”在可提取與不可提取的結構中有不同的表徵，對應於編碼關係依賴的表達與純粹連接的用法。這些結果說明了機械可解釋性如何能夠為語法提供信息，產生有關語言表徵和處理的新假設。
2604.13950.author: Sasha Boguraev et.al.
